property.name: java.library.path
property.value: C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-20\bin;C:\windows\Sun\Java\bin;C:\windows\system32;C:\windows;c:\Users\HP\AppData\Roaming\Code\User\globalStorage\github.copilot-chat\debugCommand;c:\Users\HP\AppData\Roaming\Code\User\globalStorage\github.copilot-chat\copilotCli;C:\Program Files\Common Files\Oracle\Java\javapath;C:\Program Files (x86)\Common Files\Oracle\Java\javapath;C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-11.0.15.10-hotspot\bin;C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-11.0.12.7-hotspot\bin;C:\Python310\Scripts\;C:\Python310\;C:\windows\system32;C:\windows;C:\windows\System32\Wbem;C:\windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;C:\windows\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\bin;C:\Program Files\PuTTY\;C:\Program Files\Git\cmd;C:\Program Files (x86)\Bitvise SSH Client;C:\Program Files\dotnet\;C:\Program Files\nodejs\;c:\Users\HP\AppData\Local\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin;C:\Program Files\Docker\Docker\resources\bin;C:\Program Files\MySQL\MySQL Shell 8.0\bin\;C:\Users\HP\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps;C:\Users\HP\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin;C:\Users\HP\.dotnet\tools;C:\Users\HP\AppData\Local\atom\bin;C:\Users\HP\AppData\Local\GitHubDesktop\bin;C:\Program Files\JetBrains\PyCharm Community Edition 2022.3.3\bin;;C:\intelFPGA\16.1\modelsim_ase\win32aloem;C:\Users\HP\.dotnet\tools;C:\Users\HP\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WinGet\Links;C:\Users\HP\AppData\Roaming\npm;C:\Users\HP\AppData\Local\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin;C:\Users\HP\AppData\Local\Programs\Ollama;.

testcase.name: test1_ChromeLogin
testcase.system-out:
================================================================= SCENARIO 3: Cross-Browser Consistency Testing same login on Chrome + Firefox ================================================================= 📂 Loading selector cache from: healing-cache.json 🚀 SelfHealingDriver initialized with chrome 📂 Loading selector cache from: healing-cache.json ⚠️ Firefox not available: Could not start a new session. Response code 500. Message: Expected browser binary location, but unable to find binary in default location, no 'moz:firefoxOptions.binary' capability provided, and no binary flag set on the command line Host info: host: 'LAPTOP-6U6L8IT5', ip: '192.168.56.1' Build info: version: '4.18.1', revision: 'b1d3319b48' System info: os.name: 'Windows 10', os.arch: 'amd64', os.version: '10.0', java.version: '20.0.1' Driver info: org.openqa.selenium.firefox.FirefoxDriver Command: [null, newSession {capabilities=[Capabilities {acceptInsecureCerts: true, browserName: firefox, moz:debuggerAddress: true, moz:firefoxOptions: {}}]}] Running Chrome-only test
--- Test 1: Chrome Login --- Browser: Google Chrome 🔵 🌐 Navigated to: http://localhost:3000 ✅ Found: #email-input ⌨️ Typed 'selenium@ares.com' into: email-input ✅ Found: #password-input ⌨️ Typed 'selenium123' into: password-input ✅ Found: #login-button 🖱️ Clicked: login-button Final URL: http://localhost:3000/ ✅ Chrome test passed!
testcase.system-err: [main] INFO io.github.bonigarcia.wdm.WebDriverManager - Using chromedriver 145.0.7632.117 (resolved driver for Chrome 145) [main] INFO io.github.bonigarcia.wdm.WebDriverManager - Exporting webdriver.chrome.driver as C:\Users\HP\.cache\selenium\chromedriver\win64\145.0.7632.117\chromedriver.exe Feb 27, 2026 7:25:51 PM org.openqa.selenium.devtools.CdpVersionFinder findNearestMatch WARNING: Unable to find CDP implementation matching 145 Feb 27, 2026 7:25:51 PM org.openqa.selenium.chromium.ChromiumDriver lambda$new$5 WARNING: Unable to find version of CDP to use for 145.0.7632.117. You may need to include a dependency on a specific version of the CDP using something similar to `org.seleniumhq.selenium:selenium-devtools-v86:4.18.1` where the version ("v86") matches the version of the chromium-based browser you're using and the version number of the artifact is the same as Selenium's. [main] WARN io.github.bonigarcia.wdm.WebDriverManager - Exception getting geckodriver version from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SeleniumHQ/selenium/trunk/common/geckodriver/geckodriver-support.json java.lang.NumberFormatException: Cannot parse null string at testcase.name: test2_FirefoxLogin
testcase.system-out: --- Test 2: Firefox Login --- ⚠️ Firefox not available Install Firefox to run this test Skipping gracefully...

testcase.name: test3_CompareBrowsers
testcase.system-out:
--- Test 3: Cross-Browser Comparison ---
┌──────────────────────────────────┐ │ Cross-Browser Report │ ├──────────────────────────────────┤ │ Chrome ✅ │ │ email-input → found ✅ │ │ password-input → found ✅ │ │ login-button → found ✅ │ ├──────────────────────────────────┤ │ Firefox ⚠️ Not installed │ └──────────────────────────────────┘
Same selectors work on all browsers! Self-healing works cross-browser ✅

testcase.name: test4_CrossBrowserHealing
testcase.system-out: --- Test 4: Cross-Browser Healing --- Healing same broken ID on Chrome 🌐 Navigated to: http://localhost:3000 ✅ Found: #cross-browser-email ⌨️ Typed 'crossbrowser@ares.com' into: cross-browser-email ⚕️ Chrome healing: SUCCESS ✅
